data.title: STOCKS | Southbound Funds Post Net Sell of 1.97 Billion Yuan
data.description:
Southbound funds recorded a net sell of 1.97 billion yuan today. According to Jin10, via Shanghai Stock Connect, Kingboard Laminates and Hua Hong Semiconductor saw net sells of HK$926 million and HK$677 million, respectively, while Alibaba-W led net buys at HK$896 million.

Via Shenzhen Stock Connect, SMIC and Hua Hong Semiconductor posted net sells of HK$1.21 billion and HK$1.04 billion, respectively, while GigaDevice led net buys at HK$123 million.

data.title: STOCKS | Fulaixin Materials Says Flexible Sensor Project Revenue Was 1.82 Million Yuan, With Minimal Share of Total Revenue
data.description:
Fulaixin Materials issued an abnormal trading announcement after its stock recorded a cumulative deviation of more than 20% in closing-price gains over three consecutive trading days on June 30, 2026, July 1, and July 2.

According to Jin10, the company said its main businesses include advertising inkjet printing materials, label and signage printing materials, electronic-grade functional materials, functional base film materials, and adhesive materials, among other industrial consumer products.

The company said it has noticed heightened market attention to the humanoid robot concept. After an internal review, it said that as of the disclosure date of the announcement, revenue from its flexible sensor project was 1.82 million yuan and unaudited.

It added that this revenue accounted for a very small proportion of the company’s overall revenue and had no material impact on its main business.

data.title: Beijing Tower Crash Was Deliberate Act by Pilot, Probe Finds
data.description: Chinese authorities said the pilot who crashed a small plane into Beijing’s tallest skyscraper acted deliberately, offering the first official explanation nearly a week after the incident, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Traders Focus on Supply Signals as U.S. Inventories Fall for a 10th Straight Time
data.description:
U.S. inventories fell for a 10th consecutive time, but the decline did not reverse the market’s downward trend.

According to Jin10, traders were paying more attention to resumed oil flows, Saudi Arabia’s spot sales, and expectations that OPEC+ will increase production.

data.title: River Bank & Trust Reports June 16 Ransomware Attack After Hacker Accessed Network
data.description:
River Bank & Trust said a hacker accessed its network on June 16th and deployed ransomware, as the lender reported total assets exceeding $3.8 billion.

According to NS3.AI, the bank discovered the breach on June 19th and said it is investigating the incident.

The lender is examining whether personal information was accessed or exfiltrated during the intrusion.

data.title: Japan’s Top Currency Official Mimura: Will Not Comment on Exchange Rate Levels
data.description: Japan’s top foreign exchange official, Atsushi Mimura, said he would not comment on exchange rate levels. According to Jin10, Mimura made the remarks without providing further details.

data.title: Sei Labs Releases Giga White Paper V2 With Privacy Features and Faster Finality Target
data.description:
Sei Labs has released version 2 of its Giga white paper, adding new privacy and fairness designs and lowering the Autobahn consensus protocol’s finality target from 400 milliseconds to below 250 milliseconds. According to Foresight News, the update also maintains throughput of more than 200,000 transactions per second and 5 Gigagas per second.

The revised white paper introduces the Sedna private transaction layer and a deterministic transaction ordering mechanism. Sei Labs said these changes are intended to provide pre-execution privacy, resistance to MEV, and censorship resistance.

Sei Labs said work on the Giga upgrade is ongoing. The company added that subsequent development will include a complete transaction fee mechanism, an Autobahn consensus upgrade, and new tokenomics for the SEI token.

data.title: Trump Accounts Set to Launch Next Week Without Migration Option
data.description:
The Trump administration is expected to launch “Trump Accounts” next week, but the program will not initially offer a way to migrate accounts, according to people familiar with the matter.

According to Jin10, a source told Semafor that there is currently no mechanism allowing companies to custody children’s savings accounts on their own platforms.

Institutions including Chime, Empower, Fidelity, and SoFi had hoped to quickly obtain approval from the U.S. Treasury Department to move these accounts onto their platforms. They aimed to migrate accounts from an application developed through cooperation among the Treasury Department, Bank of New York Mellon, and Robinhood in order to capture early user growth after the product launch.

One person familiar with the situation said these institutions are now relying on relevant guidance being issued no later than August.

data.title: STOCKS | Invesco Nasdaq Technology ETF to Halt Trading, Then Resume on July 3, 2026
data.description: Invesco said its Nasdaq Technology ETF will be suspended from trading starting at the market open on July 3, 2026, and will resume trading at 10:30 on July 3, 2026. According to Jin10, the fund said its redemption services will continue to operate as normal during the trading halt.

data.title: Neutral-Atom Quantum Computing Startup Nakai Quantum Raises Tens of Millions of Yuan in First Round
data.description:
Nakai Quantum (Nakai Qiyuan (Beijing) Quantum Technology Co., Ltd.), described as China’s first nK-level neutral-atom quantum computing company, has completed its first financing round worth tens of millions of yuan, led by GL Ventures with participation from Inno Angel Fund, Changshi Capital and Feitu Venture Capital, according to 36Kr. The company said the proceeds will be used to develop a next-generation neutral-atom quantum computer, build an ecosystem for an ultracold-atom quantum simulation computer, and deliver products to benchmark customers.

Founded in April 2026, Nakai Quantum said it has built a full-stack product lineup spanning complete quantum computing systems, cold/ultracold atom experimental systems, key subsystems and high-precision modular control circuits. The company said it has secured market orders worth tens of millions of yuan and forecasts full-year 2026 revenue of tens of millions of yuan.

data.title: Leeding Semiconductor Technology (Shenzhen) Files for Hong Kong IPO
data.description: Leeding Semiconductor Technology (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. has submitted a listing application to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, with CITIC Securities acting as the sole sponsor, according to 36Kr.

data.title: U.S. DOJ Charges Alleged Scattered Spider Member Peter Stokes After Extradition From Finland
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has announced criminal charges against 19-year-old U.S.-Irish dual national Peter Stokes, who is accused of being a member of the cybercrime group known as “Scattered Spider.” According to ChainCatcher, the report cited Financefeeds as saying Stokes has been extradited from Finland to the United States.

data.title: Prasad: Oil Flows Rise Through Strait of Hormuz, but Market Uncertainty Persists
data.description:
Oil extended its decline for a third day as flows through the Strait of Hormuz increased, according to Bloomberg.

Aathira Prasad, director of macroeconomics at Nasser Saidi & Associates, discussed oil supply and price prospects for the rest of 2026 in an interview with Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa.

data.title: A Scandal Over Missing Haitian Children in Chile Fueled Stricter Migration Measures
data.description:
A scandal in Chile involving claims of missing Haitian children drew widespread attention and helped set the stage for tougher migration measures, even though the allegations were largely unfounded.

Bloomberg posted on X, that the episode gained traction despite a lack of supporting evidence and later proved mostly untrue.

The controversy nonetheless influenced public debate and policy momentum, contributing to a push for stricter approaches to migration in Chile.

data.title: Ionic Digital Completes $400 Million Private Placement as AI/HPC Leasing Tops Bitcoin Mining Revenue
data.description:
Bitcoin mining firm Ionic Digital has completed a $400 million private placement financing. According to ChainCatcher, the company reported first-quarter revenue of $51.4 million this year.

Financial data showed its digital infrastructure leasing business for AI and high-performance computing generated $44 million in the quarter, surpassing revenue from its Bitcoin mining operations for the first time.

data.title: Citadel Securities Sues Former Europe Derivatives Head Leonard Lancia in London for Over £6 Million
data.description:
Citadel Securities has filed a lawsuit in London seeking more than £6 million from Leonard Lancia, its former head of European derivatives systematic market-making and a co-founder of high-frequency crypto trading firm Portofino Technologies.

According to Odaily, Citadel Securities alleges that Lancia and colleagues began planning a startup while still employed at the firm, and that Citadel previously won damages and support for legal costs in related employment arbitration.

Citadel Securities also sued Portofino Technologies in the United States in 2023, alleging theft of trade secrets. Lancia and Portofino Technologies have denied all allegations.

The London High Court rejected Lancia’s request last Friday to lift an asset-freezing order.

data.title: STOCKS | Phoenix Shipping Says Capacity And Market Share Rank In The Industry’s Lower Middle Tier
data.description:
Phoenix Shipping said its shares met the Shenzhen Stock Exchange’s criteria for abnormal trading after the stock’s cumulative closing-price deviation exceeded 20% over two consecutive sessions on July 1, 2026, and July 2, 2026.

According to Jin10, the company issued an abnormal fluctuation announcement noting that its existing fleet has a relatively small transport capacity.

Phoenix Shipping said its production capacity and market share in the shipping industry are in the lower middle tier, and that its overall business scale is limited.

data.title: STOCKS | Tech Stock Retreat Deepens Ahead of US Payrolls: Markets Wrap
data.description:
A second day of volatility in technology stocks deepened, with chipmakers in South Korea taking the brunt of the selling, according to Bloomberg.

The dollar fell and Treasuries wavered ahead of the June US payrolls report.

data.title: Eurozone May Unemployment Rate Due for Release in 10 Minutes
data.description:
The eurozone’s unemployment rate for May was due to be released in 10 minutes.

According to Jin10, the data release was imminent at the time of the report.

data.title: Standard Chartered and Circle Launch USDC Minting and Redemption Access for Institutional Clients
data.description:
Standard Chartered and Circle have jointly launched an integrated access service for institutional clients to mint and redeem USDC, allowing eligible institutions to use USDC through a single onboarding and service process without opening a separate account with Circle.

According to Foresight News, the launch makes Standard Chartered the first global systemically important bank (G-SIB) to obtain the relevant license for this service.

The service is currently available to eligible clients through Standard Chartered’s Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) operations. It combines fiat banking, digital asset infrastructure, and public blockchain networks into a bank-led solution.

Standard Chartered said the service supports institutional use cases including on-chain settlement, treasury management, and liquidity management.

The bank added that it plans to expand the service to more markets, subject to regulatory approvals and suitable market conditions.
